ocr_findings.description: First cell of UX row in the table reads 'ユース・マネ' in OCR — this text does not appear in the image at all. The cell should be the term 'UX（User Experience）' only, without any preceding garbled string. This is a spurious insertion likely from OCR hallucination of a partially-visible adjacent label.
'試験にはコレが出る！' rendered as '試験にはつしが出る！' — consistent icon-misread pattern.

reviewer_notes: その他の関連用語 table (10 terms: UX, マーチャンダイジング, ダイレクトマーケティング, etc.) plus the 長音記号 column note. The spurious 'ユース・マネ' text in the UX cell is a med-severity wrong_char issue. All other term definitions appear correct.
